forecasts.reasoning: The State of Emergency was declared on March 31, 2025, to run for 30 days, ending exactly on April 29, 2025. It was enacted due to severe power outages on Upolu island. Temporary generators from New Zealand arrived on April 5, and as of April 21, the Electric Power Corporation officially cancelled all power rationing, confirming that the generation capacity is now sufficient to meet demand. Because the immediate crisis has been resolved and rationing ended, it is highly unlikely the government will need to extend the State of Emergency. Therefore, the probability that it will still be in effect on April 30, 2025, after its scheduled expiration, is very low.

There is overwhelming, convergent evidence that Haley Stevens has either already officially announced or is effectively launching any moment before May 1, 2025. Multiple high-quality reports from mid-April (including Punchbowl News and CNN on April 10-11) confirmed she had moved up her timeline and was intending to launch "in the coming days," specifically noting she had previously planned for "the end of April" (which falls exactly before the May 1 deadline). Additionally, web search results for her verified X (Twitter) profile and campaign website (haleyformi.com) already display explicit announcement text, such as: "It's official, Michigan. I'm running to be your next United States senator" and platform details about standing up to the "Trump-Musk chaos agenda" (confirming the 2025 context). Since she has active campaign assets stating she is running and a widely-reported imminent launch window set before the end of April, a YES resolution is near certain. A very slim margin of doubt is reserved for extreme black swan events (e.g. sudden severe illness) that could delay a formal press declaration past May 1.

The original 30-day state of emergency was declared for an electricity crisis on March 31, 2025, and is scheduled to expire at the end of April 29, 2025. On April 21, 2025, Samoa's Electric Power Corporation officially cancelled all remaining power rationing because 5 large rental generators had been brought online, fully meeting the island's demand for electricity for the next 6 months. With the acute phase of the crisis resolved well before the scheduled expiration date, the most likely outcome by far is that the government will simply allow the state of emergency to lapse on April 29, 2025, without extending it into April 30. Checking current news confirms no other valid states of emergency or announced extensions.

Search results confirm that Democratic Rep. Haley Stevens has officially announced her candidacy for the U.S. Senate in Michigan. A Washington Post article dated today (April 21, 2025) is titled "Democratic Rep. Haley Stevens enters U.S. Senate race in Michigan", with the text stating "Democratic Rep. Haley Stevens is running for Michigan's open Senate seat." Furthermore, her official X (formerly Twitter) account (@HaleyForMI) features a recent post stating "It's official, Michigan. I'm running to be your next United States senator." A Punchbowl News article from April 10 indicated she was telling Democrats she was going to "imminently launch her campaign". The evidence is overwhelming and conclusive that she has formally entered the race before the May 1, 2025 deadline. The initial base rate and uncertainty are completely dominated by this direct, explicit evidence. I set the probability to the maximum allowable value.
